content.text:
<user_query>
In the codebase at /Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next, I need to understand the current "workspace" entity usage:

1. Read all files under src/routes/_authed/workspace/ 
2. Read all files under src/routes/_authed/organizations* (the organizations route and any related files)
3. Find all usages of "workspace" in the codebase - variable names, types, route paths, UI strings
4. Read the OpenAPI spec at ../anedot_next/doc/openapi.yaml (or wherever it is relative to apps/org-next) to understand the API entity names for tenants/organizations/workspaces
5. Check src/server/ for any server functions that deal with tenants, organizations, or workspaces
6. Check src/hooks/ for any hooks related to these entities
7. Look at src/components/ for workspace-related and organization-related components

I want to understand the full picture of how these three terms (organization, tenant, workspace) are currently used across the app.
</user_query>

Exploring the codebase to map how workspace, organization, and tenant are used. Gathering files and search results in parallel.

[REDACTED]

Here is a concise map of how **organization**, **tenant**, and **workspace** show up in `org-next`, with file paths and one important inconsistency to fix.

---

## 1. `src/routes/_authed/workspace/`

Only one route file:

| Path | Role |
|------|------|
| `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/workspace/new.tsx` | `/_authed/workspace/new` ‚Üí public path **`/workspace/new`**. Full-page ‚ÄúCreate your workspace‚Äù flow: name, ‚Äúworkspace type‚Äù (`org` / `vendor`), submits via `createTenant`, then `navigate({ to: "/" })`. |

Conceptually this is **UI language ‚Äúworkspace‚Äù** while the API call is still **tenant create** (`POST /tenants`).

---

## 2. `src/routes/_authed/organizations*`

Only:

| Path | Route id / path |
|------|------------------|
| `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/organizations.tsx` | `/_authed/organizations` ‚Üí **`/organizations`** |

This page:

- Reads **`tenants`** from `getRouteApi("/_authed").useLoaderData()`.
- Maps each API **tenant** to a local UI type **`Organization`** (with `orgType` ‚ÄúOrganization‚Äù or ‚ÄúVendor‚Äù).
- Uses **‚Äúworkspace‚Äù** in headings and buttons (‚ÄúYour workspaces‚Äù, ‚ÄúCreate workspace‚Äù, etc.).
- **`handleSelectOrg`** only calls `navigate({ to: "/" })` ‚Äî it does **not** set a selected tenant id (no tenant switch yet).

So: **route name** = ‚Äúorganizations‚Äù; **copy** = ‚Äúworkspaces‚Äù; **data** = `Tenant[]`.

---

## 3. ‚Äúworkspace‚Äù usages (meaningful vs noise)

**Product / routing (what you care about):**

- **Routes:** `/workspace/new` (see `routeTree.gen.ts` and `_authed.tsx`).
- **UI copy:** ‚Äúworkspace‚Äù, ‚Äúworkspace name‚Äù, ‚Äúworkspace type‚Äù, ‚ÄúCreate workspace‚Äù, ‚ÄúView your workspaces‚Äù, etc. in `workspace/new.tsx` and `organizations.tsx`.
- **Logic:** `_authed.tsx` treats ‚Äúno current tenant‚Äù and redirects to `/workspace/new` unless already on that path.
- **Comments:** `campaign-api-scope.ts` describes the **tenant** as the ‚Äúcurrent workspace tenant‚Äù for API scope.

**Noise (not the product entity):**

- **`@workspace/ui`** and other `@workspace/*` packages ‚Äî monorepo package scope, not the domain ‚Äúworkspace‚Äù.
- **`vite.config.ts`**, **`components.json`**, tests importing `@workspace/test-utils` ‚Äî same.

**Campaigns guard:** `src/routes/_authed/campaigns/-campaigns.ts` ‚Äî `"Only organization workspaces can create campaigns"` (ties **org** tenant type to the word ‚Äúworkspace‚Äù).

---

## 4. OpenAPI / API entity names

`../anedot_next/doc/openapi.yaml` **does not exist under** `/Users/duhl/git/ui` (no `anedot_next` tree and no `doc/openapi.yaml` found there).

The OpenAPI file that **does** exist and documents tenants is:

- **`/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/prototype-org-next/next-api.yaml`**

There you get first-class **`Tenant`** resources:

- Paths like **`/tenants`**, **`/tenants/{id}`**, **`/tenant_memberships`**, vendor linking, etc.
- Schemas **`Tenant`**, **`TenantCreate`**, **`Tenant`** with **`tenant_type`**, etc.  
- API description mentions **‚ÄúMulti-tenant‚Äù**; no separate top-level **‚ÄúWorkspace‚Äù** or **‚ÄúOrganization‚Äù** entity name in the same sense as **`Tenant`** ‚Äî the word ‚Äúorganization‚Äù in the app is mostly **UI** (and `tenant_type: "org"`).

So: **canonical API term = tenant**; **org** is a **tenant type**, not a separate resource name in this spec.

---

## 5. `src/server/`

| File | Tenants / org API |
|------|-------------------|
| `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/server/app-bootstrap.ts` | **`fetchTenants`** ‚Üí `GET /tenants`; builds **`AppBootstrap`** with **`currentTenant`**, **`tenant`**, **`tenants`**; **`fetchCurrentUser`** ‚Üí `/users/me`. |
| `/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/server/org-api.server.ts` | Generic **`getOrgApiJson` / `postOrgApiJson` / `putOrgApiJson`** to the org API host (no tenant-specific logic beyond HTTP). |
| Other server files (`middleware`, `session`, `error`, `rollbar`) | No tenant/workspace/organization domain logic in the grep sweep. |

**Note:** `createTenant` is **not** under `src/server/`; it lives next to routes in **`/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/-workspace.ts`** (POST `/tenants` with body `{ tenant: { name, tenant_type } }`).

---

## 6. `src/hooks/`

No hooks reference **tenant**, **organization**, or **workspace** (excluding `@workspace/*`). Either there is no `src/hooks/` usage or it‚Äôs empty for these terms.

---

## 7. `src/components/`

No dedicated **workspace** or **organization** feature components. Closest:

- **`/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/components/app-header.tsx`** ‚Äî placeholder **`currentTenantName`** / commented ‚ÄúLoading **organization**‚Äù.
- Other hits are **only** `@workspace/ui` imports.

---

## Mental model: the three terms

| Term | Role in this app today |
|------|-------------------------|
| **Tenant** | **API and TypeScript model** (`Tenant`, `TenantType`, `GET /tenants`, `tenant_id` query params, `currentTenant` in bootstrap). |
| **Workspace** | **User-facing label** and **routes** (`/workspace/new`, copy on `/organizations`). Same underlying entity as tenant. |
| **Organization** | **URL** `/organizations` and **UI label** for `tenant_type === "org"`; also **‚ÄúOrganization Settings‚Äù** on home. Not a separate API type. |

---

## Import path inconsistency (worth fixing)

- **`createTenant`** is defined in **`/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/routes/_authed/-workspace.ts`**.
- **`workspace/new.tsx`** imports **`../-tenants`** and **`organizations.tsx`** imports **`./-tenants`**, but there is **no** `-tenants.ts` file in the tree.

If the app builds, something outside this snapshot may resolve that; as checked in the repo, the filename and imports **do not match**. Aligning the import path with **`-workspace`** (or renaming the file to **`-tenants.ts`**) would remove the mismatch.

---

If you want the **exact** OpenAPI your backend team uses when it is not in this repo, point to that repo path or add `anedot_next` to the workspace and I can re-scan the same way.

[REDACTED]
